summary: Analyzed and fixed multiple issues in Table.js and Profiles.js. Root cause #1: <col> class used double-hyphen (col-col--id) while th/td class used single-hyphen (data-col-id), causing CSS rules targeting col-col-X never to match — fixed Table.js to use single-hyphen (col-col-X). Root cause #2: applyColumnWidths() skipped columns with colWidths[i]=0, meaning hidden columns stayed at their browser-default width instead of collapsing to 0 — fixed to always set the <col> width (0 for hidden, pixel for visible). Root cause #3: right-sticky offset accumulated right-to-left with flawed z-index formula — fixed to accumulate left-to-right with z-index: 10+ri. Root cause #4: right-sticky CONTROL column ignored horizontal scrollbar width — added clip-path inset on the last right-locked col to account for scrollbar. Root cause #5: autoSize only ran when explicitly set — fixed to default autoSize:true for columns without a pre-computed pixel width. Also updated Profiles.js column definitions (Proxy width 30ch→35ch, added autoSize to all non-resizable columns), added pill badge variant to Badge.css with blue/red/yellow colors, updated _renderStatusBadge to use DuckControls.Badge with pill class, and removed orphaned double-hyphen utility classes from Table.css.
current_status: Table.js and Table.css control fixes complete. Profiles.js column specs updated. Badge.css pill variant added. All linter checks pass. Ready for runtime testing.
